$comment: Ratchet manifest for the fixture-drift gate (scripts/check-fixture-drift.mjs). @openai/agents-core exposes TWO separate wire inventories the OpenAI facet consumes — kept as separate sections (per Task 6's brief) so a rename in one is attributable without conflating it with drift in the other: (1) runItemStreamEventName — the RunItemStreamEventName string-literal union from dist/events.d.ts (the `name` discriminant on every run_item_stream_event); (2) protocolItem — the top-level `type:` discriminants of the ModelItem union in dist/types/protocol.d.ts (the full bidirectional protocol-item vocabulary; a superset of OutputModelItem that also includes computer_call_result, used for input-history reconstruction). Resolved via @openai/agents (packages/e2e's exact devDependency pin) -> its own @openai/agents-core dependency, since agents-core is transitive and not directly pinned by either facet package.json. Dispositions were seeded 2026-07-03 by reading src/index.ts's drive()/driveRawResponsesEvent() switches + protocol.d.ts's per-item field shapes + the two playbook reports. handled = the facet has a dedicated, correct branch; carried = routed to ext.openai.* losslessly (the run-item switch's documented default arm); router-plane = n/a for this facet (every OpenAI event that reaches push() either maps to a concern in scope or is an already-documented ignored-duplicate of an authoritative source — noted per-entry, not a distinct control-plane class here); not-applicable = provably cannot occur on the RunItemStreamEvent/RunRawModelStreamEvent streams this facet ingests (justified per-entry — e.g. an item type only reachable via a DIFFERENT sub-union than the ones the 9 named run-item events wrap); silently-dropped = reaches the facet via an ALREADY-handled event name, carries real payload, and falls through to a generic path with no ext route. Ratchet-fix update (2026-07-03, ratchet-findings-fix task): the two `computer_call`/`computer_call_result` entries this manifest ORIGINALLY seeded as `silently-dropped` findings (the same orphan-tool.done bug class playbook-facet-adaptations-report.md's Finding #1 fixed for shell_call/apply_patch_call, left uncovered for OpenAI's Computer-Use built-in tool) are now `handled` — `builtinToolName()`/`driveBuiltinToolCalled()`/`driveBuiltinToolOutput()` in index.ts were extended to recognize both discriminants (see their own per-entry notes below). See .superpowers/sdd/pb-task-6-report.md for the full original-finding rationale and .superpowers/sdd/ratchet-findings-fix-report.md for the fix. Tool-search disposition update (2026-07-03, tool-search-disposition task): the last weak `carried` entries — the tool_search_called/tool_search_output_created run-items and the tool_search_call/tool_search_output protocol items — are now `handled`. Re-investigation against the installed agents-core 0.12.0 wire (dist/events.d.ts, dist/runner/modelOutputs.mjs + streaming.mjs + tooling.mjs) superseded playbook-facet-adaptations-report.md's Finding #4 (seeded from an older premise): the family has its OWN dedicated RunItemStreamEventName pair (not a tool_called/tool_output reuse), streams as a PAIRED call+output, and the output's `tools` array is a genuine (non-opaque) structured retrieval listing — an honest tool.start/tool.done lifecycle is supportable. `driveToolSearchCalled()`/`driveToolSearchOutput()`/`resolveToolSearchCallId()` in index.ts implement it (see their own per-entry notes below). See .superpowers/sdd/tool-search-disposition-report.md for the full rationale.

verified.evidence: fixture suite + drift gate + adversarially-verified 0.12.0->0.13.2 wire-surface audit (GPT-5.6 era: cache_write_tokens mapped, invalidFinalOutput late-message rescue; hosted multi-agent + programmatic tool calling documented KNOWN-DEFERRED); live capture pending (echo-gpt56 scenario ready)
LIVE capture: echo-gpt56 corpus (gpt-5.6) — landed as a standing replay/census seed; cache_write_tokens mapping confirmed on real wire (turn.done usage.cacheWriteTokens); zero unparsed events

fixture suite + drift gate + full 0.13.2->0.13.5 agents-core d.ts/dist diff audit: events/items/usage/protocol item shapes and the stream emitters (runner/streaming.mjs, runner/modelOutputs.mjs) byte-identical; sole wire-relevant delta is an optional camelCase itemId on the SDK-flattened output_text_delta arm (protocol.d.ts StreamEventTextStream) — deliberately no-op'd as the documented duplicate of the authoritative model:response.output_text.delta path, whose snake_case item_id is unchanged; the rest is request-side tool-definition plumbing (FunctionTool/SerializedFunctionTool providerData, explicitly stripped from tool_search_output payloads by serializeFunctionToolForToolSearchOutput) and sandbox/tracing infra

LIVE capture: echo-gpt56 standing seed REFRESHED at 0.13.5 (gpt-5.6) — the new itemId OBSERVED live on the flattened output_text_delta arm and triaged (census allowlist + field registry, first-observed provenance recorded); replay + census gates green (zero untriaged drops)

message_output_created.note: driveMessageOutputCreated() — the authoritative text-end + citations source (audit M22); refusal parts set pendingRefusal.

tool_called.note: rawItem.type==='function_call' is deliberately ignored here (superseded by the authoritative raw response.output_item.added path); rawItem.type in {shell_call, apply_patch_call, hosted_tool_call} -> driveBuiltinToolCalled() (Finding #1). See the protocolItem section for the residual computer_call gap on this SAME event name.

tool_output.note: rawItem.type in {shell_call_output, apply_patch_call_output} -> driveBuiltinToolOutput(); function_call_result -> the generic toolOutputToAgBlocks() path with structuredContent extraction. See the protocolItem section for the residual computer_call_result gap on this SAME event name.

reasoning_item_created.note: driveReasoningItemCreated() — sole source for reasoning content on this seam (start/delta/end/opaque incl. the ZDR encrypted_content replay blob).

handoff_requested.note: Opens a subagentStart bracket keyed by a synthetic turn_handoff_<n> id (audit M48 Finding 1).

handoff_occurred.note: Closes the matching subagentStart bracket (FIFO) + emits the identity-carrying `handoff` event once both agent identities are known (audit M48 Finding 1).

tool_search_called.note: FLIPPED from carried (fixture-drift ratchet disposition, 2026-07-03 — the manifest's last weak entries). Re-investigated against the installed agents-core 0.12.0 wire truth (superseding playbook-facet-adaptations-report.md's Finding #4, which reasoned from the OLDER 0.2.1-era assumption that the item had no name/input and no dedicated event; that premise no longer holds): dist/events.d.ts declares tool_search_called/tool_search_output_created as DEDICATED RunItemStreamEventName literals (NOT a tool_called/tool_output reuse, unlike shell/apply-patch/computer/hosted-tool — item 20's family); dist/runner/modelOutputs.mjs's processModelResponse[Async] push a RunToolSearchCallItem then, when the output is already resolved this SAME step (server-hosted execution returns both together, or client execution resolves synchronously via the built-in {paths} loader or an awaited custom toolSearchTool.execute()), a RunToolSearchOutputItem right after -> streaming.mjs's getRunItemStreamEventName maps each to its own name, so this is a PAIRED shape (mirrors shell_call/apply_patch_call/computer_call), not hosted_tool_call's single-shot collapse. driveToolSearchCalled() -> tool.start (name synthesized 'builtin:tool_search'; arguments, z.ZodUnknown, carried verbatim like computer_call's action/actions, never JSON-string-parsed) + registerPendingTool (Task 4b pending-tool discipline, same as shell/apply-patch/computer). Correlation is the one real wire wrinkle: UNLIKE every other builtin's REQUIRED callId, ToolSearchCallItem/ToolSearchOutputItem's call_id/callId are BOTH optional AND nullable (protocol.d.ts) — the SDK's own tooling.mjs falls back through providerData.call_id -> providerData.callId -> the item's own id -> (last resort) blind FIFO positional matching. resolveToolSearchCallId() matches the SDK's id-fallback chain exactly (providerData.call_id ?? providerData.callId ?? call_id ?? callId ?? id, providerData checked FIRST) but stops short of the FIFO fallback; a call with NO identifiable id at all degrades to ext.openai.unparsed rather than fabricating a correlation id (never reaches an orphaned tool.start). See index.ts's OpenAIToolSearchCallItem doc + SPEC §8 item 20's extended discriminant list.

tool_search_output_created.note: FLIPPED alongside tool_search_called (same disposition + citations). driveToolSearchOutput() -> tool.done: `tools` ("tool references or concrete tool definitions" per the SDK's own zod-schema doc comment — a structured retrieval listing, never natural-language text) is carried verbatim as a single AgBlock `data` block (spec's escape hatch for structured non-text tool content, mirroring computer_call_result's `file`-block treatment of its own non-text payload) — already full-fidelity, so no separate structuredContent duplicate is needed; an empty tools[] (zero matches) still produces a data block, not dropped. `status` is an UNCONSTRAINED string (unlike shell/apply-patch's closed enum) with no documented error discriminant, so — mirroring computer_call_result's identical precedent — every occurrence maps to outcome:'ok'. Correlation resolved via the same resolveToolSearchCallId() as the call side (verified against toolSearch.mjs's createClientToolSearchOutputFromTools: the client-executed built-in-loader path's output carries its correlation id ONLY in providerData.call_id, no top-level call_id/callId/id at all — resolveToolSearchCallId's providerData fallback is load-bearing for this, the SDK's own default/common Runner.run() path, not just an edge case); an output with no identifiable id degrades to ext.openai.unparsed rather than an orphaned tool.done.

protocolItem.$comment: Top-level `type:` discriminants of dist/types/protocol.d.ts's `ModelItem` union (the full bidirectional protocol-item vocabulary — a superset of OutputModelItem that also includes computer_call_result).

message.note: The rawItem of message_output_created's RunMessageOutputItem (assistant role, per the facet's own OpenAIAssistantMessageItem type) -> driveMessageOutputCreated(). ModelItem's user/system-role 'message' arms are input-side history reconstruction, not reachable on this facet's ingestion boundary (RunItemStreamEvent only ever wraps the assistant's own output).

tool_search_call.note: FLIPPED from carried (fixture-drift ratchet disposition, 2026-07-03). The rawItem of the now-dedicated tool_search_called run-item -> driveToolSearchCalled() (full tool.start/args lifecycle). See the runItemStreamEventName section's tool_search_called entry for the full wire-truth citations.

tool_search_output.note: FLIPPED from carried (fixture-drift ratchet disposition, 2026-07-03). The rawItem of the now-dedicated tool_search_output_created run-item -> driveToolSearchOutput() (tool.done with the tools listing as a `data` block). See the runItemStreamEventName section's tool_search_output_created entry for the full wire-truth citations.

hosted_tool_call.note: The rawItem of a tool_called run-item for a provider-hosted built-in (web_search/code_interpreter/file_search/image_generation/mcp/…) -> driveBuiltinToolCalled(): tool.start + tool.done fire together (hosted tools resolve server-side within the same turn, verified against agents-core 0.12.0's runner/modelOutputs.mjs — never paired with a separate tool_output).

function_call.note: Handled via the AUTHORITATIVE raw response.output_item.added stream (driveRawResponsesEvent, canonical model A1), not the run-item wrapper — the tool_called-wrapper occurrence of this SAME discriminant is a documented, deliberate no-op ('IGNORED — superseded by model:response.output_item.added') to avoid a double tool.start. Content is not lost; it arrives via the other authoritative path.

computer_call.note: FIXED (fixture-drift ratchet, 2026-07-03 — was a NEW FINDING not in either playbook report): confirmed a real arm of ToolCallItem (dist/types/protocol.d.ts) alongside shell_call/apply_patch_call/hosted_tool_call/function_call, riding the SAME already-handled `tool_called` event. `builtinToolName()` now recognizes `computer_call` explicitly (synthesized `name:"builtin:computer"`, mirroring shell_call/apply_patch_call) instead of falling back to the hosted_tool_call branch's `rawItem.name` read — ComputerUseCallItem has NO `name` field, so that fallback previously produced a malformed/undefined `name` on `a.toolStart()`. `driveBuiltinToolCalled()` treats it as a PENDING call (registerPendingTool), same as shell_call/apply_patch_call. The wire carries BOTH `action` (single action) and `actions` (batch array) — the SDK's own runtime reads `actions` FIRST (if populated), falling back to `action`. The normalizer MUST mirror this precedence: `actions ?? action ?? {}` to avoid silent loss of a batch-form call. Both fields are OPTIONAL on the wire and carried through verbatim as `tool.args.assembled`'s input without interpreting individual action fields (fixture discipline: type only what is consumed). See index.ts's `OpenAIComputerCallItem` doc + SPEC §8 item 20's extended discriminant list.

shell_call.note: The rawItem of a tool_called run-item for OpenAI's native shell built-in -> driveBuiltinToolCalled() (Finding #1); no `name` field on the wire, facet synthesizes name:'builtin:shell'.

apply_patch_call.note: The rawItem of a tool_called run-item for OpenAI's native apply-patch built-in -> driveBuiltinToolCalled() (Finding #1); no `name` field on the wire, facet synthesizes name:'builtin:apply_patch'.

function_call_result.note: The rawItem of the generic tool_output path -> toolOutputToAgBlocks() (text/input_text content) + extractStructuredContent() (the ggui cache-marker channel, two peer-supported homes).

computer_call_result.note: FIXED (fixture-drift ratchet, 2026-07-03 — was a NEW FINDING not in either playbook report): confirmed part of RunToolCallOutputItem.rawItem's widened union (FunctionCallResultItem | ComputerCallResultItem | ShellCallResultItem | ApplyPatchCallResultItem, per playbook-facet-adaptations-report.md Finding #2's own text), riding the already-handled `tool_output` event. `driveBuiltinToolOutput()` now special-cases `computer_call_result` explicitly (alongside shell_call_output/apply_patch_call_output) instead of falling through to the GENERIC function_call_result path (toolOutputToAgBlocks) — ComputerCallResultItem.output is `{type:'computer_screenshot', data:string}` (a base64 PNG screenshot), a shape toolOutputToAgBlocks's `part.type === 'text' || part.type === 'input_text'` check never matches, which previously produced content=[] (an orphaned tool.done silently discarding the screenshot — the SAME orphan-hazard bug class Finding #1 fixed for shell/apply-patch results). The fix lands the screenshot as an AgBlock `file` block (`source:{type:'base64', mediaType:'image/png', data}` — AgSource's base64 arm, agjson.ts) in tool.done.content; outcome is unconditionally `ok` (this wire arm carries no status/error discriminant, unlike shell/apply-patch results). See SPEC §8 item 20's extended discriminant list.

shell_call_output.note: driveBuiltinToolOutput() — per-command stdout/stderr joined to text content + the full array kept verbatim as structuredContent; outcome:'error' on any timeout/non-zero exit (Finding #1).

apply_patch_call_output.note: driveBuiltinToolOutput() — output string -> text content; status:'failed' -> outcome:'error' (Finding #1).

reasoning.note: The rawItem of reasoning_item_created -> driveReasoningItemCreated() (start/delta/end/opaque incl. the rs_… id and ZDR encrypted_content replay blob).

compaction.note: Not a member of ToolCallItem (5 arms: computer_call/shell_call/apply_patch_call/function_call/hosted_tool_call), the tool-output rawItem union (4 arms), AssistantMessageItem, ReasoningItem, Handoff*Item, ToolSearchCall/OutputItem, or ToolApprovalItem — i.e. not reachable via any of the 9 RunItemStreamEventName wrappers this facet's push() boundary ingests. Only appears in the broader ModelItem/OutputModelItem unions used for other purposes (e.g. full-response item-list / session-history serialization), which is outside this stream. Provably absent from the run-item event surface, not merely unhandled.

unknown.note: Same reasoning as compaction: UnknownItem (protocol.d.ts's explicit 'catch all for items that are not part of the protocol') is not a member of any of the 9 RunItemStreamEventName-wrapped rawItem sub-unions (verified: ToolCallItem's 5 arms do not include it; nor do the message/reasoning/handoff/tool-search/approval item shapes). Only reachable via the broader ModelItem/OutputModelItem unions outside this facet's ingestion boundary.
